text: A userdata object representing a world in the game.
Sets client public data on a World.
Returns client public data from a world.
Returns the id of a world.
Returns true if the world is an indoor world.
Sets public data on a World.
Returns public data from a world.
Clears the override light color on all light controllers in the world.
Destroys the given world.
params: The world that should be removed.

text: Returns the minimum and maximum cell coordinates for the given world.
return: The minimum and maximum cell coordinates (minX, maxX, minY, maxY).

text: Finds the dominant voxel material inside a capsule volume.
params: The center position of the capsule.
The height of the cylindrical part of the capsule (Z-axis aligned, excluding hemispherical caps).
return: The dominant material index value. (returns nil if no voxel was found)
The highest single-voxel density of the dominant material.
The weighted average world position of the dominant material (biased toward dense, nearby voxels)

text: Finds the dominant voxel material inside a sphere volume.
params: The center position of the sphere.
return: The dominant material index value. (returns nil if no voxel was found)
The highest single-voxel density of the dominant material.
The weighted average world position of the dominant material (biased toward dense, nearby voxels).

text: Returns the id of a world.

params: The position of the voxel chunk in the world.
return: The material index value. (returns nil if no voxel was at point)
The density value. (returns nil if no voxel was at point)

text: Get weighted voxel density in world point.
params: The world space point to check density in.
return: Weighted density. (returns 0 if world doesn't have voxels)

text: Returns the zone id from a position.
return: The Zone id. (nil if position is invalid)

text: Checks if the given cell coordinates are within the bounds of the world.
params: The X coordinate of the cell.
The Y coordinate of the cell.
return: True if the cell is within the bounds of the world, false otherwise.
If the cell is loaded or not.
If the cell is loaded or not.

text: Returns true if the world is an indoor world.
Tests if a world position is free from voxel terrain coverage.
params: The position to test for voxel coverage (e.g. shape center).
return: True if the position is clear of voxel terrain, false if covered.

text: Load a cell for player. The cell will stay loaded until the player steps into the cell.
params: A player to load for, can be nil.
Lua function to call when cell is loaded. Callback parameters are ( world, x, y, player, params )
Parameter object passed to the callback.

text: Load a cell. The cell will stay loaded until the cell is released with [LoadCellHandle].release()
or when the [LoadCellHandle] is garbage collected
params: Lua function to call when cell is loaded. Callback parameters are ( world, x, y, params )
Parameter object passed to the callback.

text: Load a cell and nav mesh. The cell will stay loaded until the cell is released with [LoadCellHandle].release()
or when the [LoadCellHandle] is garbage collected
params: Lua function to call when cell nav mesh is created. Callback parameters are ( world, x, y, params )
Parameter object passed to the callback.

text: Reload a cell. Callback result values: 0 means cell isnt active and wont be reloaded, 1 means success
params: Lua function to call when cell is reloaded. Callback parameters are ( world, x, y, result ) (Optional)

text: Sets the override light color on all light controllers in the world.
Configures the mining loot population. Matched candidates are sent to the
world script via the server_onMining callback whenever voxels are removed.
params: Spawn rules keyed by voxel material index (1-8). Each value is an array of rule tables: { sizeX, sizeY, sizeZ, seed, spawnId = integers, tunnelType = string (optional), maxDist = number (optional) }.

text: Set data to pass on to the terrain generation script. If no data is set the terrain generation script receives the same data as the world script.
params: Any data, available to the terrain generation script as parameter 6 in the create callback.

text: Modify voxel terrain with a sphere shape
params: The world position of the sphere.

text: Modify destructable terrain with a sphere shape
params: The world position of the sphere.
The materials the shape can modify. (See [sm.world.voxelFilter]). (Defaults to sm.world.voxelFilter.all)
The strength of the modification. (Defaults to radius)

Lua function to call when cell is loaded. Callback parameters are a table of destruction data { remDensities = {integer, ..}, avgPositions = {[Vec3], ..} } and (optional) pass-through params passed to this function.

text: Modify destructable terrain
params: The maximum distance from position that can be modified.
The materials the shape can modify. (See [sm.world.voxelFilter]). (Defaults to sm.world.voxelFilter.all)

text: Modify destructible terrain around a point or line with a radius.
params: The world position a of the modification.
The world direction of the modification. Non normalized direction that defines the center line of modification.
The maximum distance from positions in the line defined by position and direction that can be modified.
The materials the shape can modify. (See [sm.world.voxelFilter]). (Defaults to sm.world.voxelFilter.all)
Whether to enable destruction effect. (Defaults to true)

Lua function to call when cell is loaded. Callback parameters are a table of destruction data { remDensities = {integer, ..}, avgPositions = {[Vec3], ..} } and (optional) pass-through params passed to this function.

text: Modify destructible terrain around a point or line with a radius.
params: The world position a of the modification.
The world direction of the modification. Non normalized direction that defines the center line of modification.
The maximum distance from positions in the line defined by position and direction that can be modified.
A table of target densities to remove per material type {[1] = integer, .., [8] = integer}.
The materials the shape can modify. (See [sm.world.voxelFilter]). (Defaults to sm.world.voxelFilter.all)
Whether to enable destruction effect. (Defaults to true)

Lua function to call when cell is loaded. Callback parameters are a table of destruction data { remDensities = {integer, ..}, avgPositions = {[Vec3], ..} } and (optional) pass-through params passed to this function.

meta: Checks if two instances of [World] refer to the same World.
